title: Holy Crap They DID Change Their Minds About the Evas Souls
display_title: Holy Crap They DID Change Their Minds About the Evas Souls
wikitext:
[]: Holy Crap, They DID Change Their Minds About the Evas' Souls             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Sep 29, 2007 12:29 am http://eva.onegeek.org/pipermail/oldeva/1998-September/020217.html We all know the souls in the Evangelions are those of the pilots' mothers (with the exception of Rei), and it was almost definitely true that Yui was always intended to be inside Unit 01. But this article shows almost conclusively that it wasn't always intended that all the Evangelions had mothersouls within them. A quote here from the third episode's original script: EVANGELION ORIGINAL I wrote: Toji (on his sister) : "She didn't die, but now she's stuck in the hospital. My father and my mother both work at the institute; they can't get out of the workplace right now . . . If I'm not there to look after her, who will? She'd be so lonely."

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Sep 29, 2007 12:52 am I never understood why it has to be a mother. Why can't it be any sort of loved one, like a father or sibling?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Sep 29, 2007 12:54 am The CI outright states that it's always the mother, except in Rei's case. Both Shinji's and Asuka's mothers are in the Evas, and all of those eligible to be Childrens are apparently motherless. I think it's always been assumed to be the mother before that, anyway, but I could be wrong, as I didn't get into NGE until after the CI had come out.

[]:             Quote Post by Avatar of Dreams » Sat Sep 29, 2007 1:00 am BrikHaus wrote: I never understood why it has to be a mother. Why can't it be any sort of loved one, like a father or sibling?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Sep 29, 2007 1:05 am NAveryW wrote: The CI outright states that it's always the mother

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Sep 29, 2007 1:14 am Hey, your POV is your POV. Though from what I understand, the CI stuff in the game is simply meant to go into further detail on what we already know about the series. *shrugs* I see your point, but I guess part of Eva is how Anno wanted it to be a "thinking" anime and all... Anyway, what I find interesting with the EML post Naw-kun pointed out was this: EVANGELION ORIGINAL II: [Episode 18: The choice of his life / AMBIVALENCE] Neon Genesis Evangelion #18-17 ****************************************************** Sun-lit hospital room (morning) The morning sun shines in through the shutters. Toji is in uniform, standing beside his sister's bed. Toji: "I've decided . . . Even though I won't be able to see you for a long time, but because you'll be able to go to a better hospital . . . so . . ." Insert get-well present. Toji: "Tomorrow you'll be moved to the new hospital. That's all . . . goodbye." Toji picks up his luggage. (the camera doesn't include his sister)

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Sep 29, 2007 7:02 am Eva was not planned out before they started making it. Things like this are bound to crop up. > adds this to his notes for future reference.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Sep 29, 2007 9:42 am BrikHaus wrote: I never understood why it has to be a mother.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Sat Sep 29, 2007 9:54 am BrikHaus wrote: [ It seems more like additional information created to whore the series rather than something Anno felt compelled to write to clear up the mysteries of his show.

[]:             Quote Post by Incisivis » Sat Sep 29, 2007 11:34 am BrikHaus wrote: But my question is why? Why does it have to be the mother?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Sep 29, 2007 11:41 am From an interview with Sadamoto in the Deluxe edition of Der Mond Sadamoto wrote: And just about that time, I was watching the NHK [public TV channel] program "Brain and Heart" and learned about the existence of the A10 nerve, and I told Anno about the idea that popped into my head at that time. That was the idea where "the dead mother is inside the robot, which is operated by mental/psychical bonding with the child. Moreover, parent-child relations are parched/strained due to the death of the mother at a young age."

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Sep 29, 2007 12:26 pm How come I've never read that before?!?! Hmm, I kind of feel like I have now, but as I've said before, I do get deja vu all the time. I do definitely remember reading about the A-10 nerve, though. I also find it interesting that it was Sadamoto's idea and not Anno's.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Sep 29, 2007 10:01 pm Sorry for the double post, but I Googled the phrase "the dead mother is inside the robot" and found this . Reading that, I realize that Sadamoto had WAY more input into Evangelion than most people seem to give him credit for. He not only came up with the mothersoul thing and the character designs, but he came up with the title itself . We know from the "Anno talks to kids" thing that Anno chose the name Evangelion because it sounded complicated, but I now know that 'twas Sadamoto who proposed it. Now the main thought on my mind is that NGE's development was a joint collaboration between Hideaki Anno and Yoshiyuki Sadamoto, and Anno fleshed out the idea and the characters' psyches and such. This probably explains the manga. There seems to be a lot of valuable information hidden within the depths of that archive, but there's just so much fluff in there too that it's hard to bring myself to look through everything to find it all... EDIT: It may not initially seem like the title affects the storyline that much, but consider that "Evangelion" wasn't just the title for the show, but the mecha. Now, without that name, there wouldn't be the parallel of Eva being created from Adam. Thus, it seems likely that the whole idea of Christian symbolism was Sadamoto's.

[]: Kind of Off Topic, but who cares!             Quote Post by TriLink » Sat Sep 29, 2007 10:41 pm NAveryW wrote: I realize that Sadamoto had WAY more input into Evangelion than most people seem to give him credit for. He not only came up with the mothersoul thing and the character designs, but he came up with the title itself .

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Sep 29, 2007 10:53 pm Sweet this I like. I feel even better about calling kaworu a bastard now

[]: Re: Kind of Off Topic, but who cares!             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Sun Sep 30, 2007 8:30 am TriLink12 wrote: NAveryW wrote: I realize that Sadamoto had WAY more input into Evangelion than most people seem to give him credit for. He not only came up with the mothersoul thing and the character designs, but he came up with the title itself .

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Sep 30, 2007 10:11 am "The initial title was Alcion"...it means "Kingfisher", or otherwise called a "Halcyon". Legend has it that when the Halcyon nests, the sea shall be calm and tranquil and no storms can come. Of course, the Katakana could go for "Allusion", and there's plenty of that in Eva. (BobBQ, remember everyone's favorite Halcyon-class Cruiser? ) Ooooo...Ed Stalker and Alec Freeman from UFO? My my my, we have a solid addition to the wiki here! Well, it shows that Sadamoto and Anno did do a lot of changes through the course of the Opus. Rei's bandages were Sadamoto's idea? Of course, so was pen-pen! And Hideaki Anno may not have said, "I picked Evangelion because it sounded sophisticated" but rather, "Evangelion was chosen because it sounded sophisticated". More like Lost in Translation than a false statment.

Article: Holy Crap They DID Change Their Minds About the Evas Souls
...Ed Stalker and Alec Freeman from UFO? My my my, we have a solid addition to the wiki here! Well, it shows that Sadamoto and Anno did do a lot of changes through the course of the Opus. Rei's bandages were Sadamoto's idea? Of course, so was pen-pen! And Hideaki Anno may not have said, "I picked Evangelion because it sounded sophisticated" but rather, "Evangelion was chosen because it sounded sophisticated". More like Lost in Translation than a false statment.
